THE CITY OF OKLAHOMA AND LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA REPORT BUILDING DAMAGE NEAR AND AROUND STOCKYARD CITY. SEVERAL BUILDINGS SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. MUCH OF THE DAMAGE WAS THE RESULT OF ROOF DEBRIS BLOWN INTO OTHER STRUCTURES. SEVERAL TREES...A STEEL BILLBOARD... AND AT LEAST 12 POWER POLES WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN. ADDITIONAL MINOR DAMAGE WAS REPORTED NEAR MERIDIAN AND I-40 AT THE WHITE WATER WATER PARK. THREE MINOR INJURIES WERE REPORTED THERE.

SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED WITH HEAVY TREE DAMAGE
SEVERAL 80-FOOT TRANSMISSION POLES AND LINES WERE DOWNED. FOUR TO SIX HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE AS THE LINES FELL.
A LARGE PORTION OF THE ROOF AT THE LUTHERAN CHURCH WAS DESTROYED.
SEVERAL LARGE BARNS WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED. SEVERAL POWER POLES AND TREES WERE SNAPPED OR BLOWN DOWN.
FENCE PANELS AND LARGE LIMBS BLOWN DOWN

THE ROOF AT THE HOSPITAL SUSTAINED DAMAGE. ONE OTHER HOME SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE. NUMEROUS POWER POLES AND LINES ARE DOWN OVER A 4 TO 5 BLOCK AREA. POWER WAS KNOCKED OUT TO MUCH OF THE CITY. THE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.
POWER POLES BLOWN DOWN
NUMEROUS POWER POLES DOWN AND ESTIMATED 70 MPH WINDS
A TREE WAS KNOCKED OVER AND SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN ON THE CAMPUS OF OKLAHOMA STATE UNIVERSITY.

SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN, WITH NUMEROUS OTHER SMALLER BRANCHES. PART OF THE FACADES WAS RIPPED OFF OF TWO BUSINESSES. POWER WAS KNOCKED FOR TO OVER 3800 RESIDENTS. ALSO, SOME OF THE DEBRIS KNOCKED DOWN POWER LINES, WHICH CAUSED A FEW STRUCTURE FIRES. ONE HOUSE WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED BY THE FIRE.
POWER POLES DOWN, TRANSFORMERS BLOWN, LARGE TREE BRANCHES BROKEN. TWO SEMI TRAILERS BLOWN OVER ON I-40 3 MILES WEST OF TOWN. WIND ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.
A MOBILE HOME WAS BLOWN OVER ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF TOWN.

ONE HOME SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE. AT LEAST 40 OTHER BUILDINGS SUSTAINED SOME FORM OF DAMAGE, ABOUT HALF OF WHICH WERE HOMES. AN AWNING AT A GAS STATION WAS BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL TREES AND LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN. NUMEROUS FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN. A LARGE POWER POLE, IN ADDITION TO SEVERAL OTHER SMALLER POWER POLES AND LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. THE MAJORITY OF TOWN WAS WITHOUT POWER FOR SEVERAL HOURS.

A HOUSE LOST 90 PERCENT OF ITS ROOF. THE ROOF WAS SCATTERED APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS TO THE EAST. A METAL BARN EAST OF THE HOUSE WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. OTHER BARNS AND FARM OUTBUILDINGS TO THE EAST ALSO SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE.
OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED 60 TO 70 MPH WIND GUSTS

THE OKLAHOMA COUNTY EMERGENCY MANGEMENT SURVEYED DAMAGE NEAR ROCKWELL AVENUE AND MEMORIAL ROAD. ONE HAME SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. A LARGE POWER POLE WAS SHATTERED. ELEVEN OTHER POWER POLES AND ASSOCIATED LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN. THE DAMAGE EXTENDED ABOUT A HALF OF A MILE TO THE SOUTH, WITH DAMAGE EXTENDING OUTWARD ABOUT 100 YARDS ON EITHER SIDE OF THE ROCKWELL AVENUE.
